title: Terminate TLS with a Kubernetes Secret
description:
## Terminate TLS with a Kubernetes Secret

HAProxy Ingress can terminate TLS using a certificate stored in a Kubernetes `Secret` of type `kubernetes.io/tls`. The Ingress `spec.tls` block references the secret by name.

**Your task:**

In the `haproxy-lab` namespace:
1. Create a TLS Secret named `app-tls` using the self-signed certificate and key provided below (use the literal values — no real cert required for this exercise).
2. Create an `Ingress` named `tls-ingress` that:
   - Uses `ingressClassName: haproxy`
   - Terminates TLS for host `app.example.com` using the `app-tls` secret
   - Routes requests for host `app.example.com` with path `/` (`pathType: Prefix`) to a Service named `app-svc` on port `80`

```bash
# Verify secret type:
kubectl get secret app-tls -n haproxy-lab -o jsonpath='{.type}'
# Verify TLS host on Ingress:
kubectl get ingress tls-ingress -n haproxy-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.tls[0].hosts[0]}'
```

hints.title: Creating a TLS secret
hints.body: Use kubectl create secret tls to create the secret from cert/key files. For this exercise use --cert and --key pointing to any files, or create it from literals with --from-literal.
hints.command: kubectl create secret tls app-tls --cert=/dev/null --key=/dev/null -n haproxy-lab 2>/dev/null || kubectl create secret generic app-tls --type=kubernetes.io/tls --from-literal=tls.crt='' --from-literal=tls.key='' -n haproxy-lab

hints.title: Ingress with spec.tls block
hints.body: The spec.tls[] array references the Secret and lists the hostnames it covers. The spec.rules[] must have a matching host entry.
hints.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: tls-ingress
  namespace: haproxy-lab
spec:
  ingressClassName: haproxy
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - app.example.com
    secretName: app-tls
  rules:
  - host: app.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: app-svc
            port:
              number: 80
EOF

validation.description: Check that the TLS Secret and Ingress are correctly configured

commands.description: Secret app-tls exists with type kubernetes.io/tls
commands.command: kubectl get secret app-tls -n haproxy-lab -o jsonpath='{.type}'
kubectl get ingress tls-ingress -n haproxy-lab -o jsonpath='{.metadata.name}'
kubectl get ingress tls-ingress -n haproxy-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.tls[0].hosts[0]}'
kubectl get ingress tls-ingress -n haproxy-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.tls[0].secretName}'
kubectl get ingress tls-ingress -n haproxy-lab -o jsonpath='{.spec.ingressClassName}'
